meta.title: ASEAN VAT/GST/PPN Rates
meta.description: Standard and reduced VAT/GST/PPN rates for all 10 ASEAN member states

rates.notes: Brunei does not impose VAT, GST, or any general consumption tax.
Auto-updated from PwC on 2026-05-01. Previous rate: 10%. Standard 10% VAT on most goods and services. Exports and certain supplies are zero-rated.
Auto-updated from PwC on 2026-05-01. Previous rate: 12%. PPN increased from 11% to 12% effective 1 January 2025 per UU HPP. Certain luxury goods subject to additional PPnBM (luxury goods sales tax).
Standard 10% VAT. Exports are zero-rated. Certain basic goods and services are exempt.
Malaysia replaced GST with SST in September 2018. Sales tax is 5% or 10% on goods; service tax is 8% (increased from 6% effective 1 March 2024). Not a VAT system.
Auto-updated from PwC on 2026-05-01. Previous rate: 5%. Myanmar levies commercial tax (similar to VAT) at 5% on most goods and services. Gold, jewelry, and certain vehicles taxed at higher rates.

Auto-updated from PwC on 2026-05-01. Previous rate: 12%. Standard 12% VAT on sale/import of goods and services. Exports are zero-rated. Certain basic goods, services and small businesses (below PHP 3M threshold) are VAT-exempt.
GST increased from 8% to 9% effective 1 January 2024 (second phase of the planned increase from 7%). Exports and international services are zero-rated.

rates.local_name: ภาษีมูลค่าเพิ่ม (VAT)
rates.notes: Statutory rate is 10% but a royal decree reduces it to 7% (has been continuously extended). Exports are zero-rated.

Auto-updated from PwC on 2026-05-01. Previous rate: 10%. Standard 10% VAT rate. Reduced 5% rate applies to essential goods (clean water, medical equipment, teaching aids, etc.). Exports are zero-rated.
